rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RACHEL ROBBINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Release Of Property From Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 9, 2021 line 1. PETITIONERS RACHEL ROBBINS AND IAN MCFARLAND'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN is continued to September 15, 2021. No later than August 28, 2021 moving party to file a supplemental brief discussing the propriety of proceeding with this action while another action addressing the validity of the mechanics' lien is pending. Respondent shall file a response, if any, to the supplemental brief no later than September 10, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALYSSA MUELLER VS. ELITE INVESTMENT & CAPITAL GROUP INC. A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 9, 2021 line 3. NOTICE OF JOINDER BY DEFENDANTS ELITE INVESTMENT & CAPITAL GROUP INC, EDWARD C. WONG, MATTHEW XU, AND VINCY ZHAO IN DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) as to each element of this cause of action as to each defendant. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment - tort is overruled. Moving party acknowledges that tort damages are available for breach of this covenant under certain circumstances and does not cite any authorities for the proposition that recovery under the circumstances of this case is barred. Demurrer to the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress is overruled. Moving party does not argue that no cause of action is alleged and appears to take issue with the label. Allegations within this cause of action appear to be no more that negligence claim with request for damages; a claim Defendant asserts is proper under Molien v. Kaiser (1980) 27 Cal.3d 916. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER ROEBUCK VS. VERTEX PROPERTY GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 9, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER ROEBUCK'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED without prejudice for Plaintiff to promptly file a Motion for Leave to Amend in compliance with CRC 3.1324 and reflecting the facts and procedural posture of this case. (Points and Authorities reference filing of a Second Amended Complaint while this is a motion for leave to file a First Amended Complaint). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
